rss.title: Home working, long leases and rise of parking apps - what went wrong for NCP
rss.snippet: How could a company that charged as much as £65 for a day's parking fail to turn a profit?

rss.title: Work from home and drive more slowly to save energy, global body urges
rss.snippet: People should change how they travel, work and cook to tackle the energy price crisis, the International Energy Agency says.

rss.title: UK borrowing costs hit highest level since 2008 financial crisis
rss.snippet: The interest rate on government debt is climbing over fears about inflation, interest rates, and public spending, experts have said.

arxiv_papers.title: A Survey of Reinforcement Learning For Economics
arxiv_papers.snippet: This survey (re)introduces reinforcement learning methods to economists. The curse of dimensionality limits how far exact dynamic programming can be effectively applied, forcing us to rely on suitably "small" problems or our ability to convert "big" problems into smaller ones. arxiv_papers.title: The Data-Dollars Tradeoff: Privacy Harms vs. Economic Risk in Personalized AI Adoption
arxiv_papers.snippet: Privacy concerns significantly impact AI adoption, yet little is known about how information environments shape user responses to data leak threats. We conducted a 2 x 3 between-subjects experiment (N=610) examining how risk versus ambiguity about privacy leaks affects the adoption of AI personaliza

podcast: [The little pet fish that saved a town in the Amazon] The cardinal tetra is one of the most popular pet fish in the world. They look like little red and blue sequins. You've almost certainly seen them at the pet store or the fish tank at your dentist's office. They're everywhere. Not so long ago, most of the world's supply of cardinals came from just one place. It's a little town deep in the rainforests of Brazil, where locals still catch these fish
